focus: correctness: logic correctness & state-machine edge cases

title: setGoal can revive stale terminal state and carry budgets into replacement goals
detail: setGoal computes tokenBudget from current?.tokenBudget before branching, then the same-objective branch spreads the current record while forcing status back to active. After a budget_limited or complete goal, /goal set with the same objective preserves stale tokensUsed, tokenBudget, verifier, continuation, and completion fields; a different replacement objective also inherits the old budget when tokenBudget is omitted.
claim: A normal /goal set can produce an active goal that still carries terminal-state accounting or an unintended previous budget, causing immediate budget stops or stale verifier/completion state.
counterevidenceSought: I checked whether the command or tests reset terminal fields or pass an explicit null tokenBudget on replacement; goal.md only passes objective for set, and the state tests cover active same-objective idempotence but not terminal reactivation.
alternativeExplanation: The budget inheritance may have been intended as a sticky session setting, but the same-objective terminal reactivation still leaves active records with terminal counters and stale verifier metadata.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the product contract explicitly says /goal set same-objective is only an idempotent resume of the exact same goal, including preserving exhausted budgets and verifier state, and users must /goal clear before restarting.
suggestedFix: Create a fresh goal when current.status is not active, reset usage/verifier/completion/continuation fields when reactivating, and only inherit tokenBudget for an active same-objective update or when the caller explicitly asks for inheritance.

title: Usage dedupe only remembers the immediately previous message
detail: accountUsage skips a message only when messageID equals current.lastAccountedMessageID, then overwrites that field with the latest charged id. The lifecycle test proves adjacent replay is skipped, but an interleaved A/B/A message.updated order charges A twice and can prematurely trip budget_limited.
claim: Repeated message.updated events are not reliably deduped unless duplicates are contiguous, so token accounting can be silently inflated in normal streaming or delayed-update scenarios.
counterevidenceSought: I looked for a stronger OpenCode ordering guarantee or a test covering interleaved duplicate updates; the local lifecycle spec explicitly calls out repeated message.updated events, and the test only replays the same message immediately.
alternativeExplanation: If OpenCode guarantees a session can never emit a duplicate message.updated after any other message id has been observed, the single-slot dedupe is sufficient.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if runtime documentation or live tracing proves duplicate message.updated events are always contiguous and message ids are always present for usage-bearing events.
suggestedFix: Persist a bounded set of accounted message ids per goal, or store per-message cumulative totals and add only deltas; skip budget accounting for usage-bearing events without a stable message id.

title: Unsupported-verb contract conflicts with bare-text routing
detail: The command contract promises unsupported verbs emit STATUS=FAIL, but the execution order routes every other non-empty QUERY to mk_goal set. That makes command-like typos such as /goal delete create a goal objective instead of failing as documented.
claim: The command markdown has a router contract drift that can surprise users and tests because unsupported verbs and bare objectives are not distinguishable under the current rules.
counterevidenceSought: I checked whether goal.md defines a reserved verb list or a syntax rule for distinguishing bare objectives from unsupported verbs; it does not.
alternativeExplanation: The intended behavior may be that all unknown text is an objective and line 30 is stale documentation.
downgradeTrigger: Drop if line 30 is removed or the command intentionally documents unknown tokens as objective text with no unsupported-verb failure mode.
suggestedFix: Either remove the unsupported-verb failure promise, or add explicit reserved-verb handling so unknown command-like verbs fail while ordinary bare objectives still set the goal.
